focus: Security deep-dive for PR 5 metrics labels, PR 2 settings command strings, PR 4 freshness narrowing, PR 7 parity assertions, and hook path resolution

title: PR-5 skill_id metric labels are repo-controlled strings with no cardinality clamp
reproduce: Create many filesystem skill graph-metadata.json files or SQLite skill_nodes rows with unique skill_id/id values, enable SPECKIT_METRICS_ENABLED=true, then call scoreAdvisorPrompt(); SpeckitMetricsCollector keys each distinct skill_id directly via speckitSeriesKey.
fix: Normalize skill_id to a bounded vocabulary or bucket before emission, and make SpeckitMetricsCollector enforce declared label value policies.

title: Claude hook commands execute a repo-relative hook selected by ambient cwd and PATH
reproduce: From a nested Git repository that contains its own .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/hooks/claude/user-prompt-submit.js, run the command literal from .claude/settings.local.json:31; git rev-parse resolves the nested repo and executes that repo-relative hook path.
fix: Anchor hook commands to an absolute canonical project root or resolve relative to the settings file location, and use a pinned Node executable or validated PATH contract.

evidence: Env-derived runtime and freshness_state labels still flow from process.env.SPECKIT_RUNTIME and process.env.SPECKIT_ADVISOR_FRESHNESS in fusion.ts:320-322, and runtime also flows into structural-indexer.ts:1419-1422; the collector keys labels directly in metrics.ts:578-590.
trust-state.ts:59 still only exports TrustState = SkillGraphTrustState; GraphFreshness and StructuralReadiness remain exported from ops-hardening.ts:7-8 through code-graph surfaces.
settings-driven-invocation-parity.vitest.ts:135 still wraps all production settings assertions in describe.skipIf(!isClaudeCodeRuntime()), so non-Claude default test runs skip the F23.1 guard.
